title.zh: 卷二十八上 地理志
title.raw: 卷二十八上 地理志 Volume 28a: Treatise on Geography 1

sentences.zh: 昔在黃帝，作舟車以濟不通，旁行天下，方制萬里，畫野分州，得百里之國萬區。

translations.literal: In former times under the Yellow Emperor, boats and carts were made to cross what could not be crossed; routes spread across the realm, standards reached ten thousand li, the land was mapped and divided into provinces, yielding ten thousand districts of hundred-li states.
translations.idiomatic: In former times under the Yellow Emperor, boats and carts were made to cross what could not be crossed; routes spread across the realm, standards reached ten thousand li, the land was mapped and divided into provinces, yielding ten thousand districts of hundred-li states.

sentences.zh: 是故易稱「先王以建萬國，親諸侯」，《書》云「協和萬國」，此之謂也。

translations.literal: Thus the Changes says, "Former kings established myriad states and kept feudal lords close," and the Documents says, "They harmonized the myriad states"—this is what it means.
translations.idiomatic: Thus the Changes says, "Former kings established myriad states and kept feudal lords close," and the Documents says, "They harmonized the myriad states"—this is what it means.

sentences.zh: 堯遭洪水，褱山襄陵，天下分絕，為十二州，使禹治之。

translations.literal: When Yao faced the great flood, waters embraced mountains and overtopped hills, splitting the realm apart. He made twelve provinces and had Yu regulate them.
translations.idiomatic: When Yao faced the great flood, waters embraced mountains and overtopped hills, splitting the realm apart. He made twelve provinces and had Yu regulate them.

sentences.zh: 水土既平，更制九州，列五服，任土作貢。

translations.literal: Once waters and lands were leveled, he recast them as nine provinces, set out five service zones, and assigned tribute according to local produce.
translations.idiomatic: Once waters and lands were leveled, he recast them as nine provinces, set out five service zones, and assigned tribute according to local produce.

sentences.zh: 曰：禹敷土，隨山刊木，奠高山大川。

translations.literal: It says: Yu spread out the land, followed mountains and cut paths through woods, and fixed the high mountains and great rivers.
translations.idiomatic: It says: Yu spread out the land, followed mountains and cut paths through woods, and fixed the high mountains and great rivers.

sentences.zh: 冀州既載，壺口治梁及岐。

translations.literal: When Jizhou was set in order, Hukou was regulated and Liang and Qi reached.
translations.idiomatic: When Jizhou was set in order, Hukou was regulated and Liang and Qi reached.

sentences.zh: 既脩太原，至于嶽陽。

translations.literal: Taiyuan was repaired, reaching to the south of Mount Yue.
translations.idiomatic: Taiyuan was repaired, reaching to the south of Mount Yue.

sentences.zh: 覃懷底績，至于衡章。

translations.literal: Tanhuai was brought to completion, reaching Hengzhang.
translations.idiomatic: Tanhuai was brought to completion, reaching Hengzhang.

sentences.zh: 厥土惟白壤。

translations.literal: Its soil was white loam.
translations.idiomatic: Its soil was white loam.

sentences.zh: 厥賦上上錯，厥田中中。

translations.literal: Its levy was highest-high mixed rate; its fields were middle-middle.
translations.idiomatic: Its levy was highest-high mixed rate; its fields were middle-middle.

sentences.zh: 恆、衛既從，大陸既作。

translations.literal: The Heng and Wei rivers followed their courses; the Great Plain was restored.
translations.idiomatic: The Heng and Wei rivers followed their courses; the Great Plain was restored.

sentences.zh: 鳥夷皮服。

translations.literal: The Bird Yi wore skins as clothing.
translations.idiomatic: The Bird Yi wore skins as clothing.

sentences.zh: 夾右碣石，入于河。

translations.literal: Passing right of Jieshi cliffs, one entered the Yellow River.
translations.idiomatic: Passing right of Jieshi cliffs, one entered the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 泲、河惟兗州。

translations.literal: Ji and Yellow formed Yanzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Ji and Yellow formed Yanzhou.

sentences.zh: 九河既道，雷夏既澤，雍、沮會同，桑土既蠶，是降丘宅土。

translations.literal: The Nine Rivers were channeled, Leixia became marsh, Yong and Ju met and joined; mulberry lands raised silkworms, and people descended from hills to settle plains.
translations.idiomatic: The Nine Rivers were channeled, Leixia became marsh, Yong and Ju met and joined; mulberry lands raised silkworms, and people descended from hills to settle plains.

sentences.zh: 厥土黑墳，屮繇木條。

translations.literal: Its soil was dark, rich, and mounded; grasses and trees were lush and straight.
translations.idiomatic: Its soil was dark, rich, and mounded; grasses and trees were lush and straight.

sentences.zh: 厥田中下，賦貞，作十有三年乃同。

translations.literal: Its fields were middle-lower, levy fixed; after thirteen years they matched the standard.
translations.idiomatic: Its fields were middle-lower, levy fixed; after thirteen years they matched the standard.

sentences.zh: 厥貢漆絲，厥棐織文。

translations.literal: Its tribute was lacquer and silk, with patterned woven goods.
translations.idiomatic: Its tribute was lacquer and silk, with patterned woven goods.

sentences.zh: 浮于泲、漯，通于河。

translations.literal: They floated down Ji and Tuo to reach the Yellow River.
translations.idiomatic: They floated down Ji and Tuo to reach the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 海、岱惟青州。

translations.literal: Sea and Tai formed Qingzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Sea and Tai formed Qingzhou.

sentences.zh: 嵎夷既略，惟、甾其道。

translations.literal: Yuyi was surveyed; the Wei and Zai rivers found their channels.
translations.idiomatic: Yuyi was surveyed; the Wei and Zai rivers found their channels.

sentences.zh: 厥土白墳，海瀕廣潟。

translations.literal: Its soil was white and rich, with broad saline flats by the sea.
translations.idiomatic: Its soil was white and rich, with broad saline flats by the sea.

sentences.zh: 田上下，賦中上。

translations.literal: Fields ranked upper-lower; levy middle-upper.
translations.idiomatic: Fields ranked upper-lower; levy middle-upper.

sentences.zh: 貢鹽、絺，海物惟錯，岱畎絲、枲、鈆、松、怪石，萊夷作牧，厥棐览絲。

translations.literal: Tribute included salt and fine cloth, various sea products, silk, hemp, lead, pine, and rare stones from Tai valleys; Lai Yi herded stock, and their tribute included dark silk floss.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute included salt and fine cloth, various sea products, silk, hemp, lead, pine, and rare stones from Tai valleys; Lai Yi herded stock, and their tribute included dark silk floss.

sentences.zh: 浮于汶，達于泲。

translations.literal: They floated down Wen and reached Ji.
translations.idiomatic: They floated down Wen and reached Ji.

sentences.zh: 海、岱及淮惟徐州。

translations.literal: Sea, Tai, and Huai formed Xuzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Sea, Tai, and Huai formed Xuzhou.

sentences.zh: 淮、沂其乂，蒙、羽其藝。

translations.literal: Huai and Yi were brought to order; Meng and Yu were cultivated.
translations.idiomatic: Huai and Yi were brought to order; Meng and Yu were cultivated.

sentences.zh: 大野既豬，東原厎平。

translations.literal: The Great Marsh was impounded and the eastern plain leveled.
translations.idiomatic: The Great Marsh was impounded and the eastern plain leveled.

sentences.zh: 厥土赤埴墳，草木漸包。

translations.literal: Its soil was red clay-rich loam, with grasses and trees increasingly dense.
translations.idiomatic: Its soil was red clay-rich loam, with grasses and trees increasingly dense.

sentences.zh: 田上中，賦中中。

translations.literal: Fields were upper-middle; levy middle-middle.
translations.idiomatic: Fields were upper-middle; levy middle-middle.

sentences.zh: 貢土五色，羽畎夏狄，嶧陽孤桐，泗瀕浮磬，淮夷蠙珠臮魚，厥棐玄纖縞。

translations.literal: Tribute was five-colored earth, bird plumes and summer pelts from valleys, lone paulownia from Yiyang, floating chimes from Si shores, pearls and fish from Huai Yi, and dark fine silk-gauze goods.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute was five-colored earth, bird plumes and summer pelts from valleys, lone paulownia from Yiyang, floating chimes from Si shores, pearls and fish from Huai Yi, and dark fine silk-gauze goods.

sentences.zh: 浮于淮、泗，達于河。

translations.literal: They floated down Huai and Si and reached the River.
translations.idiomatic: They floated down Huai and Si and reached the River.

sentences.zh: 淮、海惟揚州。

translations.literal: Huai and sea formed Yangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Huai and sea formed Yangzhou.

sentences.zh: 彭蠡既豬，陽鳥逌居。

translations.literal: When Poyang was impounded, migratory birds settled there.
translations.idiomatic: When Poyang was impounded, migratory birds settled there.

sentences.zh: 三江既入，震澤厎定。

translations.literal: The Three Rivers entered their courses and Zhen Marsh was stabilized.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Rivers entered their courses and Zhen Marsh was stabilized.

sentences.zh: 篠簜既敷，魇夭木喬。

translations.literal: Bamboo and reeds spread; scrub and tall trees rose.
translations.idiomatic: Bamboo and reeds spread; scrub and tall trees rose.

sentences.zh: 厥土塗泥。

translations.literal: Its soil was muddy silt.
translations.idiomatic: Its soil was muddy silt.

sentences.zh: 田下下，賦下上錯。

translations.literal: Fields were lower-lower; levy lower-upper mixed.
translations.idiomatic: Fields were lower-lower; levy lower-upper mixed.

sentences.zh: 貢金三品，瑤、鲥、篠簜，齒、革、羽毛，鳥夷卉服，厥棐織貝，厥包橘、柚，錫貢。

translations.literal: Tribute included three grades of metal, jade and fish products, bamboo and reeds, teeth, hides, feathers, and hair; Bird Yi wore plant fiber clothing; their woven tribute included shell-cloth; bundled oranges and pomelos were given by special command.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute included three grades of metal, jade and fish products, bamboo and reeds, teeth, hides, feathers, and hair; Bird Yi wore plant fiber clothing; their woven tribute included shell-cloth; bundled oranges and pomelos were given by special command.

sentences.zh: 均江海，通于淮、泗。

translations.literal: They equalized routes by river and sea, connecting to Huai and Si.
translations.idiomatic: They equalized routes by river and sea, connecting to Huai and Si.

sentences.zh: 荊及衡陽惟荊州。

translations.literal: Jing and south of Heng formed Jingzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Jing and south of Heng formed Jingzhou.

sentences.zh: 江、漢朝宗于海。

translations.literal: The Yangtze and Han paid court to the sea.
translations.idiomatic: The Yangtze and Han paid court to the sea.

sentences.zh: 九江孔殷，沱、灊既道，雲夢，土作乂。

translations.literal: The Nine Rivers were abundant; Tuo and Qian were channeled; Yunmeng lands were brought into order.
translations.idiomatic: The Nine Rivers were abundant; Tuo and Qian were channeled; Yunmeng lands were brought into order.

sentences.zh: 厥土塗泥。

translations.literal: Its soil was muddy silt.
translations.idiomatic: Its soil was muddy silt.

sentences.zh: 田下中，賦上下。

translations.literal: Fields were lower-middle; levy upper-lower.
translations.idiomatic: Fields were lower-middle; levy upper-lower.

sentences.zh: 貢羽旄、齒、革，金三品，杶、幹、栝、柏，厲、砥、砮、丹，惟箘廐、楛，三國厎貢厥名，包匭菁茅，厥棐玄纁璣組，九江納錫大龜。

translations.literal: Tribute included feathers, yak tails, teeth, hides, three grades of metal, various timbers, whetstones, arrowstones, cinnabar, special bamboo and thorn woods, and produce from three states; bundled green thatch, dark-red woven jewels and cords, and from Nine Rivers a specially offered great turtle.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute included feathers, yak tails, teeth, hides, three grades of metal, various timbers, whetstones, arrowstones, cinnabar, special bamboo and thorn woods, and produce from three states; bundled green thatch, dark-red woven jewels and cords, and from Nine Rivers a specially offered great turtle.

sentences.zh: 浮于江、沱、灊、漢，逾于洛，至于南河。

translations.literal: They floated by Yangtze, Tuo, Qian, and Han, crossed to Luo, and reached the southern Yellow River.
translations.idiomatic: They floated by Yangtze, Tuo, Qian, and Han, crossed to Luo, and reached the southern Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 荊、河惟豫州。

translations.literal: Jing and River formed Yuzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Jing and River formed Yuzhou.

sentences.zh: 伊、雒、瀍、澗既入于河，滎、波既豬，道荷澤，被盟豬。

translations.literal: Yi, Luo, Chan, and Jian entered the River; Ying and Bo were impounded; waters reached He marsh and spread to Meng marsh.
translations.idiomatic: Yi, Luo, Chan, and Jian entered the River; Ying and Bo were impounded; waters reached He marsh and spread to Meng marsh.

sentences.zh: 厥土惟壤，下土墳壚。

translations.literal: Its soil was good loam, with lower lands of mounded black earth.
translations.idiomatic: Its soil was good loam, with lower lands of mounded black earth.

sentences.zh: 田中上，賦錯上中。

translations.literal: Fields were middle-upper; levy mixed upper-middle.
translations.idiomatic: Fields were middle-upper; levy mixed upper-middle.

sentences.zh: 貢漆、枲、絺、紵、棐纖纊，錫貢磬錯。

translations.literal: Tribute included lacquer, hemp, fine cloth, ramie, fine floss goods, and specially offered chime-stones of mixed type.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute included lacquer, hemp, fine cloth, ramie, fine floss goods, and specially offered chime-stones of mixed type.

sentences.zh: 浮于洛，入于河。

translations.literal: They floated down Luo and entered the River.
translations.idiomatic: They floated down Luo and entered the River.

sentences.zh: 華陽、黑水惟梁州。

translations.literal: South of Hua and Blackwater formed Liangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: South of Hua and Blackwater formed Liangzhou.

sentences.zh: 岷、嶓既藝，沱、灊既道，蔡、蒙旅平，和夷厎績。

translations.literal: Min and Bo were cultivated; Tuo and Qian were channeled; Cai and Meng districts leveled and Yi peoples made productive.
translations.idiomatic: Min and Bo were cultivated; Tuo and Qian were channeled; Cai and Meng districts leveled and Yi peoples made productive.

sentences.zh: 厥土青黎。

translations.literal: Its soil was blue-black.
translations.idiomatic: Its soil was blue-black.

sentences.zh: 田下上，賦下中三錯。

translations.literal: Fields were lower-upper; levy lower-middle with three mixed rates.
translations.idiomatic: Fields were lower-upper; levy lower-middle with three mixed rates.

sentences.zh: 貢璆、鐵、銀、鏤、砮、磬，熊、羆、狐、貍、織皮。

translations.literal: Tribute included jade, iron, silver, metalwork, arrowstones, chime-stones, and hides of bears, foxes, and civets.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute included jade, iron, silver, metalwork, arrowstones, chime-stones, and hides of bears, foxes, and civets.

sentences.zh: 西頃因桓是來，浮于灊，逾于沔，入于渭，亂于河。

translations.literal: From Xiqing by way of Huan came products from Kunlun, Xizhi, and Qusou; western Rong were brought into order.
translations.idiomatic: From Xiqing by way of Huan came products from Kunlun, Xizhi, and Qusou; western Rong were brought into order.

sentences.zh: 黑水、西河惟雍州。

translations.literal: Blackwater and west of the River formed Yongzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Blackwater and west of the River formed Yongzhou.

sentences.zh: 弱水既西，涇屬渭汭。

translations.literal: Weakwater turned west; Jing joined the Wei confluence.
translations.idiomatic: Weakwater turned west; Jing joined the Wei confluence.

sentences.zh: 漆、沮既從，酆水逌同。

translations.literal: Qi and Ju followed course; Feng waters joined together.
translations.idiomatic: Qi and Ju followed course; Feng waters joined together.

sentences.zh: 荊、岐既旅，終南、惇物，至于鳥鼠。

translations.literal: Jing and Qi mountains were traversed, from Zhongnan and Dunwu to Bird-Mouse mountain.
translations.idiomatic: Jing and Qi mountains were traversed, from Zhongnan and Dunwu to Bird-Mouse mountain.

sentences.zh: 原隰厎績，至于豬野。

translations.literal: Plains and wetlands were brought to completion, reaching Zhuye.
translations.idiomatic: Plains and wetlands were brought to completion, reaching Zhuye.

sentences.zh: 三危既宅，三苗丕敘。

translations.literal: Three-Wei were settled and the Three Miao greatly ordered.
translations.idiomatic: Three-Wei were settled and the Three Miao greatly ordered.

sentences.zh: 厥土黃壤。

translations.literal: Its soil was yellow loam.
translations.idiomatic: Its soil was yellow loam.

sentences.zh: 田上上，賦中下。

translations.literal: Fields were upper-upper; levy middle-lower.
translations.idiomatic: Fields were upper-upper; levy middle-lower.

sentences.zh: 貢球、琳、琅玕。

translations.literal: Tribute included jade globes, fine jades, and langgan gems.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute included jade globes, fine jades, and langgan gems.

sentences.zh: 浮于積石，至于龍門西河，會于渭汭。

translations.literal: They floated from Jishi to Longmen and the western Yellow River, joining at the Wei confluence.
translations.idiomatic: They floated from Jishi to Longmen and the western Yellow River, joining at the Wei confluence.

sentences.zh: 織皮昆崙、析支、渠叟，西戎即敘。

translations.literal: From woven hides came products of Kunlun, Xizhi, and Qusou; western Rong came into orderly submission.
translations.idiomatic: From woven hides came products of Kunlun, Xizhi, and Qusou; western Rong came into orderly submission.

sentences.zh: 道钐及岐，至于荊山，逾于河；

translations.literal: Following Min and Qi, they reached Jing mountain and crossed the River;
translations.idiomatic: Following Min and Qi, they reached Jing mountain and crossed the River;

sentences.zh: 壺口、雷首，至于大嶽；

translations.literal: from Hukou and Leishou they reached Great Yue;
translations.idiomatic: from Hukou and Leishou they reached Great Yue;

sentences.zh: 厎柱、析城，至于王屋；

translations.literal: from Dizhu and Xicheng they reached Wangwu;
translations.idiomatic: from Dizhu and Xicheng they reached Wangwu;

sentences.zh: 太行、恆山，至于碣石，入于海。

translations.literal: from Taihang and Heng they reached Jieshi and entered the sea.
translations.idiomatic: from Taihang and Heng they reached Jieshi and entered the sea.

sentences.zh: 西傾、朱圉、鳥鼠，至于太華；

translations.literal: From Xiqing, Zhuyu, and Bird-Mouse they reached Great Hua;
translations.idiomatic: From Xiqing, Zhuyu, and Bird-Mouse they reached Great Hua;

sentences.zh: 熊耳、外方、桐柏，至于倍尾。

translations.literal: from Xionger, Waifang, and Tongbai they reached Beiwei.
translations.idiomatic: from Xionger, Waifang, and Tongbai they reached Beiwei.

sentences.zh: 道嶓冢，至于荊山；

translations.literal: Following Bo mound they reached Jing mountain;
translations.idiomatic: Following Bo mound they reached Jing mountain;

sentences.zh: 內方，至于大別；

translations.literal: from Neifang they reached Great Bie;
translations.idiomatic: from Neifang they reached Great Bie;

sentences.zh: 崏山之陽，至于衡山，過九江，至于敷淺原。

translations.literal: from south of Min mountain they reached Heng mountain, crossed Nine Rivers, and reached Fuzhanyuan.
translations.idiomatic: from south of Min mountain they reached Heng mountain, crossed Nine Rivers, and reached Fuzhanyuan.

sentences.zh: 道弱水，至于合藜，餘波入于流沙。

translations.literal: They guided Weakwater to Heli; remaining flow entered the shifting sands.
translations.idiomatic: They guided Weakwater to Heli; remaining flow entered the shifting sands.

sentences.zh: 道黑水，至于三危，入于南海。

translations.literal: They guided Blackwater to Three-Wei and into the southern sea.
translations.idiomatic: They guided Blackwater to Three-Wei and into the southern sea.

sentences.zh: 道河積石，至于龍門，南至于華陰，東至于厎柱，又東至于盟津，東過洛汭，至于大伾，北過降水，至于大陸，又北播為九河，同為逆河，入于海。

translations.literal: They guided the River from Jishi to Longmen, south to Huayin, east to Dizhu, further east to Mengjin, east past Luo confluence to Dapi, north past Jiang waters to the Great Plain, then north split into Nine Rivers, together called Reverse River, entering the sea.
translations.idiomatic: They guided the River from Jishi to Longmen, south to Huayin, east to Dizhu, further east to Mengjin, east past Luo confluence to Dapi, north past Jiang waters to the Great Plain, then north split into Nine Rivers, together called Reverse River, entering the sea.

sentences.zh: 嶓冢道漾，東流為漢，又東為滄浪之水，過三澨，至于大別，南入于江，東匯澤為彭蠡，東為北江，入于海。

translations.literal: From Bo mound they guided Yang waters east as Han, then east as Canglang waters, past the three shoals to Great Bie, south into Yangtze, east gathering as Poyang, east as North River into the sea.
translations.idiomatic: From Bo mound they guided Yang waters east as Han, then east as Canglang waters, past the three shoals to Great Bie, south into Yangtze, east gathering as Poyang, east as North River into the sea.

sentences.zh: 崏山道江，東別為沱，又東至于醴，過九江，至于東陵，東迆北會于匯，東為中江，入于海。

translations.literal: From Min mountain they guided Yangtze, branching east as Tuo, then east to Li, past Nine Rivers to Dongling, winding east and north to join at Hui, then east as Middle River into the sea.
translations.idiomatic: From Min mountain they guided Yangtze, branching east as Tuo, then east to Li, past Nine Rivers to Dongling, winding east and north to join at Hui, then east as Middle River into the sea.

sentences.zh: 道沇水，東流為泲，入于河，軼為滎，東出于陶丘北，又東至于荷，又東北會於汶，又北東入于海。

translations.literal: They guided Yan waters east as Ji into the River, overflowed as Ying, then east out north of Taoqiu, east to He, northeast to meet Wen, then north-east into the sea.
translations.idiomatic: They guided Yan waters east as Ji into the River, overflowed as Ying, then east out north of Taoqiu, east to He, northeast to meet Wen, then north-east into the sea.

sentences.zh: 道淮自桐柏，東會于泗、沂，東入于海。

translations.literal: They guided Huai from Tongbai, east to meet Si and Yi, then east into the sea.
translations.idiomatic: They guided Huai from Tongbai, east to meet Si and Yi, then east into the sea.

sentences.zh: 道渭自鳥鼠同穴，東會于酆，又東至于涇，又東過漆、沮，入于河。

translations.literal: They guided Wei from Bird-Mouse common cave, east to meet Feng, then east to Jing, then east past Qi and Ju into the River.
translations.idiomatic: They guided Wei from Bird-Mouse common cave, east to meet Feng, then east to Jing, then east past Qi and Ju into the River.

sentences.zh: 道洛自熊耳，東北會于澗、瀍，又東會于伊，又東北入于河。

translations.literal: They guided Luo from Xionger, northeast to meet Jian and Chan, then east to meet Yi, then northeast into the River.
translations.idiomatic: They guided Luo from Xionger, northeast to meet Jian and Chan, then east to meet Yi, then northeast into the River.

sentences.zh: 九州逌同，四奧既宅，九山刊旅，九川滌原，九澤既陂，四海會同。

translations.literal: The nine provinces were thus harmonized; the four remote regions settled; the nine mountains traversed; the nine rivers dredged to source; the nine marshes embanked; and the four seas brought to common order.
translations.idiomatic: The nine provinces were thus harmonized; the four remote regions settled; the nine mountains traversed; the nine rivers dredged to source; the nine marshes embanked; and the four seas brought to common order.

sentences.zh: 六府孔修，庶土交正，厎慎財賦，咸則三壤，成賦中國。

translations.literal: The six treasuries were fully regulated, all soils correctly arranged, levies carefully managed, all by three soil grades, thereby completing tribute assessments of the central realm.
translations.idiomatic: The six treasuries were fully regulated, all soils correctly arranged, levies carefully managed, all by three soil grades, thereby completing tribute assessments of the central realm.

sentences.zh: 錫土姓：「祗台德先，不距朕行。」

translations.literal: He granted lands and surnames, saying: "Reverently uphold and exalt virtue first; do not reject my course."
translations.idiomatic: He granted lands and surnames, saying: "Reverently uphold and exalt virtue first; do not reject my course."

sentences.zh: 五百里甸服：百里賦內總，二百里納銍，三百里內戛服，四百里粟，五百里米。

translations.literal: Five hundred li of Dian service: first hundred li paid bundled grain stalks, second hundred delivered cut grain, third hundred delivered husked grain, fourth hundred delivered grain, fifth hundred delivered rice.
translations.idiomatic: Five hundred li of Dian service: first hundred li paid bundled grain stalks, second hundred delivered cut grain, third hundred delivered husked grain, fourth hundred delivered grain, fifth hundred delivered rice.

sentences.zh: 五百里侯服：百里采，二百里男國，三百里諸侯。

translations.literal: Five hundred li of Hou service: first hundred li were cai-fiefs, second hundred li were male states, third hundred li were feudal states.
translations.idiomatic: Five hundred li of Hou service: first hundred li were cai-fiefs, second hundred li were male states, third hundred li were feudal states.

sentences.zh: 五百里綏服：三百里揆文教，二百里奮武衛。

translations.literal: Five hundred li of Sui service: three hundred li measured by civil instruction, two hundred li by martial defense.
translations.idiomatic: Five hundred li of Sui service: three hundred li measured by civil instruction, two hundred li by martial defense.

sentences.zh: 五百里要服：三百里夷，二百里蔡。

translations.literal: Five hundred li of Yao service: three hundred li Yi, two hundred li Cai.
translations.idiomatic: Five hundred li of Yao service: three hundred li Yi, two hundred li Cai.

sentences.zh: 五百里荒服：三百里蠻，二百里流。

translations.literal: Five hundred li of Huang service: three hundred li Man, two hundred li exile zones.
translations.idiomatic: Five hundred li of Huang service: three hundred li Man, two hundred li exile zones.

sentences.zh: 東漸于海，西被于流沙，朔、南臮，聲教訖于四海。

translations.literal: To the east it reached the sea, to the west covered the shifting sands, to north and south alike, and the sound of teaching extended to the four seas.
translations.idiomatic: To the east it reached the sea, to the west covered the shifting sands, to north and south alike, and the sound of teaching extended to the four seas.

sentences.zh: 禹錫玄圭，告厥成功。

translations.literal: Yu received the dark jade tablet and announced his success.
translations.idiomatic: Yu received the dark jade tablet and announced his success.

sentences.zh: 後受禪於虞，為夏后氏。

translations.literal: He later received abdication from Yu and became founder of the Xia house.
translations.idiomatic: He later received abdication from Yu and became founder of the Xia house.

sentences.zh: 殷因於夏，亡所變改。

translations.literal: Shang inherited from Xia and changed nothing substantial.
translations.idiomatic: Shang inherited from Xia and changed nothing substantial.

sentences.zh: 周既克殷，監於二代而損益之，定官分職，改禹徐、梁二州合之於雍、青，分冀州之地以為幽、并。

translations.literal: After Zhou had conquered Yin, it took lessons from the previous two dynasties, reducing and augmenting their institutions, fixed offices and divided duties, merged Yu's Xu and Liang Provinces into Yong and Qing, and split Ji Province to create You and Bing.
translations.idiomatic: After overthrowing the Shang, the Zhou court adapted earlier institutions, reorganized offices and responsibilities, folded Xu and Liang into Yong and Qing, and carved You and Bing out of Ji Province.

sentences.zh: 故周官有職方氏，掌天下之地，辯九州之國。

translations.literal: Therefore in Zhou administration there was the Director of Regions, who managed the lands under Heaven and distinguished the states of the nine provinces.
translations.idiomatic: So the Zhou established an office in charge of territorial administration, tasked with classifying the states of the nine provinces.

sentences.zh: 東南曰揚州：其山曰會稽，藪曰具區，川曰三江，寖曰五湖；

translations.literal: The southeast is called Yang Province: its mountain is Kuaiji, its marsh is Juqu, its rivers are the Three Rivers, its inundations are the Five Lakes.
translations.idiomatic: The southeastern region was Yang Province, marked by Mount Kuaiji, the Juqu marsh, the Three Rivers, and the Five Lakes.

sentences.zh: 其利金、錫、竹箭；

translations.literal: Its resources are gold, tin, and bamboo arrows.
translations.idiomatic: Its chief products were gold, tin, and bamboo for arrows.

sentences.zh: 民二男五女；

translations.literal: Its people are two men to five women.
translations.idiomatic: Its population ratio was recorded as two men for every five women.

sentences.zh: 畜宜鳥獸，穀宜稻。

translations.literal: Its livestock are suited to birds and beasts, and its grain is suited to rice.
translations.idiomatic: The area favored fowl and game, and its staple grain was rice.

sentences.zh: 正南曰荊州：其山曰衡，藪曰雲夢，川曰江、漢，寖曰潁、湛；

translations.literal: Due south is called Jing Province: its mountain is Heng, its marsh is Yunmeng, its rivers are the Yangtze and Han, its inundations are the Ying and Zhan.
translations.idiomatic: Directly south lay Jing Province, defined by Mount Heng, the Yunmeng marshes, the Yangtze and Han rivers, and the Ying and Zhan waters.

sentences.zh: 其利丹、銀、齒、革；

translations.literal: Its resources are cinnabar, silver, ivory, and hides.
translations.idiomatic: Its noted products were cinnabar, silver, ivory, and leather.

sentences.zh: 民一男二女；

translations.literal: Its people are one man to two women.
translations.idiomatic: Its recorded sex ratio was one man to two women.

sentences.zh: 畜及穀宜，與揚州同。

translations.literal: Its livestock and grains are suitable, the same as Yang Province.
translations.idiomatic: Its livestock and crops matched those of Yang Province.

sentences.zh: 河南曰豫州：其山曰華，藪曰圃田，川曰滎、雒，簧曰波、溠；

translations.literal: South of the Yellow River is called Yu Province: its mountain is Hua, its marsh is Putian, its rivers are Ying and Luo, its watercourses are Bo and Cha.
translations.idiomatic: The region south of the Yellow River was Yu Province, with Mount Hua, the Putian marsh, the Ying and Luo rivers, and the Bo and Cha waterways.

sentences.zh: 其利林、漆、絲枲；

translations.literal: Its resources are timber, lacquer, silk, and hemp.
translations.idiomatic: Its valuable products included timber, lacquer, silk, and hemp.

sentences.zh: 民二男三女；

translations.literal: Its people are two men to three women.
translations.idiomatic: Its ratio was listed as two men for every three women.

sentences.zh: 畜宜六擾，其穀宜五種。

translations.literal: Its livestock suit the six domestic animals; its grains suit the five kinds.
translations.idiomatic: It supported all six domestic animals and all five principal grains.

sentences.zh: 正東曰青州：其山曰沂，藪曰孟諸，川曰淮、泗，寖曰沂、沭；

translations.literal: Due east is called Qing Province: its mountain is Yi, its marsh is Mengzhu, its rivers are Huai and Si, its inundations are Yi and Shu.
translations.idiomatic: To the east was Qing Province, identified with Mount Yi, the Mengzhu marsh, the Huai and Si rivers, and the Yi and Shu waters.

sentences.zh: 其利蒲、魚；

translations.literal: Its resources are reeds and fish.
translations.idiomatic: Its chief products were reeds and fish.

sentences.zh: 民二男三女；

translations.literal: Its people are two men to three women.
translations.idiomatic: Its recorded ratio was two men to three women.

sentences.zh: 其畜宜雞、狗，穀宜稻、麥。

translations.literal: Its livestock suit chickens and dogs; its grains suit rice and wheat.
translations.idiomatic: It was suited to raising chickens and dogs, and to growing rice and wheat.

sentences.zh: 河東曰兗州：其山曰岱，藪曰泰野，其川曰河、泲，浸曰盧、濰；

translations.literal: East of the Yellow River is called Yan Province: its mountain is Tai, its marsh is Taiye, its rivers are the Yellow River and Ji, its inundations are Lu and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: East of the Yellow River lay Yan Province, with Mount Tai, the Taiye marsh, the Yellow and Ji rivers, and the Lu and Wei waters.

sentences.zh: 其利蒲、魚；

translations.literal: Its resources are reeds and fish.
translations.idiomatic: Its key products were reeds and fish.

sentences.zh: 民二男三女；

translations.literal: Its people are two men to three women.
translations.idiomatic: Its ratio was two men for every three women.

sentences.zh: 其畜宜六擾，穀宜四種。

translations.literal: Its livestock suit the six domestic animals; its grains suit four kinds.
translations.idiomatic: It supported all six domestic animals and four major grains.

sentences.zh: 正西曰雍州：其山曰嶽，藪曰弦蒲，川曰涇、汭，其浸曰渭、洛；

translations.literal: Due west is called Yong Province: its mountain is Yue, its marsh is Xianpu, its rivers are Jing and Rui, its inundations are Wei and Luo.
translations.idiomatic: To the west was Yong Province, marked by Mount Yue, the Xianpu marsh, the Jing and Rui rivers, and the Wei and Luo waters.

sentences.zh: 其利玉、石；

translations.literal: Its resources are jade and stone.
translations.idiomatic: Its valued materials were jade and stone.

sentences.zh: 其民三男二女；

translations.literal: Its people are three men to two women.
translations.idiomatic: Its ratio was recorded as three men to two women.

sentences.zh: 畜宜牛、馬，穀宜黍、稷。

translations.literal: Its livestock suit oxen and horses; its grains suit millet and broomcorn millet.
translations.idiomatic: It favored cattle and horses, and grew millet and broomcorn millet.

sentences.zh: 東北曰幽州：其山曰醫無閭，藪曰豯養，川曰河、泲，浸曰菑、時；

translations.literal: Northeast is called You Province: its mountain is Yiwulu, its marsh is Xiyang, its rivers are the Yellow River and Ji, its inundations are Zi and Shi.
translations.idiomatic: In the northeast was You Province, with Mount Yiwulu, the Xiyang marsh, the Yellow and Ji rivers, and the Zi and Shi waters.

sentences.zh: 其利魚、鹽；

translations.literal: Its resources are fish and salt.
translations.idiomatic: Its chief products were fish and salt.

sentences.zh: 民一男三女；

translations.literal: Its people are one man to three women.
translations.idiomatic: Its recorded ratio was one man to three women.

sentences.zh: 畜宜四擾，穀宜三種。

translations.literal: Its livestock suit four domestic animals; its grains suit three kinds.
translations.idiomatic: It was suitable for four types of domestic animals and three principal grains.

sentences.zh: 河內曰冀州：其山曰霍，藪曰揚紆，川曰漳，浸曰汾、潞；

translations.literal: Within the Yellow River is called Ji Province: its mountain is Huo, its marsh is Yangyu, its river is Zhang, its inundations are Fen and Lu.
translations.idiomatic: The inner Yellow River region was Ji Province, marked by Mount Huo, the Yangyu marsh, the Zhang River, and the Fen and Lu waters.

sentences.zh: 其利松、柏；

translations.literal: Its resources are pine and cypress.
translations.idiomatic: Its valued timber was pine and cypress.

sentences.zh: 民五男三女；

translations.literal: Its people are five men to three women.
translations.idiomatic: Its ratio was listed as five men to three women.

sentences.zh: 畜宜牛、羊，穀宜黍、稷。

translations.literal: Its livestock suit oxen and sheep; its grains suit millet and broomcorn millet.
translations.idiomatic: It favored cattle and sheep, with millet and broomcorn millet as staple crops.

sentences.zh: 正北曰并州：其山曰恆山，藪曰昭餘祁，川曰虖池、嘔夷，浸曰淶、易；

translations.literal: Due north is called Bing Province: its mountain is Mount Heng, its marsh is Zhaoyuqi, its rivers are Huchi and Ouyi, its inundations are Lai and Yi.
translations.idiomatic: To the north was Bing Province, with Mount Heng, the Zhaoyuqi marsh, the Huchi and Ouyi rivers, and the Lai and Yi waters.

sentences.zh: 其利布帛；

translations.literal: Its resources are cloth and silk fabrics.
translations.idiomatic: Its notable products were cloth and silk textiles.

sentences.zh: 民二男三女；

translations.literal: Its people are two men to three women.
translations.idiomatic: Its ratio was two men for every three women.

sentences.zh: 畜宜五擾，穀宜五種。

translations.literal: Its livestock suit five domestic animals; its grains suit five kinds.
translations.idiomatic: It supported five kinds of livestock and all five major grains.

sentences.zh: 而保章氏掌天文，以星土辯九州之地，所封封域皆有分星，以視吉凶。

translations.literal: The Grand Scribe in charge of celestial signs administered astronomy, using stellar territories to distinguish the lands of the nine provinces; all enfeoffed domains had corresponding stars, by which fortune and misfortune were observed.
translations.idiomatic: The court astronomer matched each of the nine provinces to its assigned stars; every fief had a celestial counterpart used to read auspicious and ominous signs.

sentences.zh: 周爵五等，而土三等：公、侯百里，伯七十里，子、男五十里。

translations.literal: Zhou had five ranks of nobility, while lands had three grades: dukes and marquises had one hundred li, earls had seventy li, viscounts and barons had fifty li.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou nobility had five ranks, but fiefs came in three standard sizes: 100 li for dukes and marquises, 70 for earls, and 50 for viscounts and barons.

sentences.zh: 不滿為附庸，蓋千八百國。

translations.literal: Those below that were dependent states; in all there were about 1,800 states.
translations.idiomatic: Smaller polities were treated as dependencies, bringing the total to roughly 1,800 states.

sentences.zh: 而太昊、黃帝之後，唐、虞侯伯猶存，帝王圖籍相踵而可知。

translations.literal: And the descendants of Taihao and Yellow Emperor, and the lords and earls of Tang and Yu, still existed; imperial and royal records followed one after another and can be known.
translations.idiomatic: Descendant lines from legendary rulers and from Tang and Yu still survived, and the successive records of emperors and kings make this traceable.

sentences.zh: 周室既衰，禮樂征伐自諸侯出，轉相吞滅，數百年間，列國秏盡。

translations.literal: After the Zhou house declined, rites, music, campaigns, and punishments issued from the feudal lords; they swallowed one another, and over several hundred years the listed states were exhausted.
translations.idiomatic: Once Zhou authority weakened, power shifted to the feudal lords, who conquered one another until the old roster of states was nearly wiped out over a few centuries.

sentences.zh: 至春秋時，尚有數十國，五伯迭興，總其盟會。

translations.literal: By the Spring and Autumn period there were still several dozen states; the Five Hegemons rose in turn and led their alliances.
translations.idiomatic: In Spring and Autumn times, dozens of states remained, and the Five Hegemons took turns dominating interstate alliances.

sentences.zh: 陵夷至於戰國，天下分而為七，合從連衡，經數十年。

translations.literal: Declining further into the Warring States, the realm split into seven; vertical and horizontal alliances lasted for decades.
translations.idiomatic: By the Warring States era, the world had fractured into seven powers that maneuvered through shifting alliance blocs for decades.

sentences.zh: 秦遂并兼四海。

translations.literal: Qin then annexed and united all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Qin eventually conquered and unified the empire.

sentences.zh: 以為周制微弱，終為諸侯所喪，故不立尺土之封，分天下為郡縣，盪滅前聖之苗裔，靡有孑遺者矣。

translations.literal: It considered Zhou institutions weak and ultimately lost to the feudal lords, so it established no enfeoffed lands even one chi wide, divided the realm into commanderies and counties, and swept away the descendants of former sages, leaving virtually none.
translations.idiomatic: Judging Zhou-style enfeoffment too weak, Qin refused to grant hereditary fiefs, reorganized the realm into commanderies and counties, and all but eradicated the old noble lineages.

sentences.zh: 漢興，因秦制度，崇恩德，行簡易，以撫海內。

translations.literal: When Han arose, it followed Qin institutions, esteemed grace and virtue, and practiced simplicity and ease to pacify the empire.
translations.idiomatic: When the Han was founded, it largely inherited Qin institutions but governed with lighter, more humane policies to settle the realm.

sentences.zh: 至武帝攘卻胡、越，開地斥境，南置交阯，北置朔方之州，兼徐、梁、幽、并夏、周之制，改雍曰涼，改梁曰益，凡十三郡，置刺史。

translations.literal: By Emperor Wu, he repelled Hu and Yue, opened lands and expanded frontiers, established Jiaozhi in the south and Shuofang province in the north, combined the Xia and Zhou arrangements of Xu, Liang, You, and Bing, changed Yong to Liang and Liang to Yi; in all thirteen provinces were set with regional inspectors.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Wu, campaigns against the Xiongnu and Yue expanded Han territory. Jiaozhi was established in the south and Shuofang in the north; provincial lines were adjusted, and thirteen inspection regions were ultimately set up under cishi inspectors.

sentences.zh: 先王之跡既遠，地名又數改易，是以采獲舊聞，考跡詩書，推表山川，以綴禹貢、周官、春秋，下及戰國、秦、漢焉。

translations.literal: Since the traces of former kings are distant and place names have repeatedly changed, therefore old reports were collected, tracks in the Odes and Documents examined, mountains and rivers inferred and tabulated, to connect the Yu Tribute, Zhou Offices, and Spring and Autumn, down to Warring States, Qin, and Han.
translations.idiomatic: Because ancient records were remote and place names had shifted repeatedly, this account gathers old traditions, checks them against the Classics, and maps mountains and rivers from the Yu Gong and Zhou institutions down through the Warring States, Qin, and Han.

sentences.zh: 京兆尹，元始二年戶十九萬五千七百二，口六十八萬二千四百六十八。

translations.literal: Capital Commandant (Jingzhao Yin): in Yuanshi year 2, households 195,702; population 682,468.
translations.idiomatic: Jingzhao Commandery (capital district): in 2 CE, it had 195,702 households and a population of 682,468.

sentences.zh: 縣十二：長安，新豐，船司空，藍田，華陰，鄭，湖，下邽，南陵，奉明，霸陵，杜陵。

translations.literal: Counties (12): Chang'an, Xinfeng, Chuan Sikong, Lantian, Huayin, Zheng, Hu, Xiagui, Nanling, Fengming, Baling, Duling.
translations.idiomatic: Counties (12): Chang'an, Xinfeng, Chuansikong, Lantian, Huayin, Zheng, Hu, Xiagui, Nanling, Fengming, Baling, and Duling.

sentences.zh: 左馮翊，戶二十三萬五千一百一，口九十一萬七千八百二十二。

translations.literal: Left Fengyi: households 235,101; population 917,822.
translations.idiomatic: Zuo Fengyi had 235,101 households and 917,822 people.

sentences.zh: 縣二十四：高陵，櫟陽，翟道，池陽，夏陽，衙，粟邑，谷口，

translations.literal: Counties (24): Gaoling, Liyang, Zhaidao, Chiyang, Xiayang, Ya, Suyi, Gukou,
translations.idiomatic: Counties (24): Gaoling, Liyang, Zhaidao, Chiyang, Xiayang, Ya, Suyi, Gukou,

sentences.zh: 蓮勺，頻陽，臨晉，重泉，郃陽，祋祤，武城，沈陽，褱德，徵，

translations.literal: Lianshao, Pinyang, Linjin, Chongquan, Heyang, Deyu, Wucheng, Shenyang, Huaide, Zheng,
translations.idiomatic: Additional counties: Lianshao, Pinyang, Linjin, Chongquan, Heyang, Deyu, Wucheng, Shenyang, Huaide, Zheng.

sentences.zh: 雲陵，萬年，長陵，陽陵，雲陽。

translations.literal: Yunling, Wannian, Changling, Yangling, Yunyang.
translations.idiomatic: Additional counties: Yunling, Wannian, Changling, Yangling, and Yunyang.

sentences.zh: 右扶風，戶二十一萬六千三百七十七，口八十三萬六千七十。

translations.literal: Right Fufeng: households 216,377; population 836,070.
translations.idiomatic: You Fufeng had 216,377 households and a population of 836,070.

sentences.zh: 縣二十一：渭城，槐里，鄠，盩厔，斄，郁夷，美陽，郿，雍，漆，栒邑，隃麋，陳倉，杜陽，汧，好畤，

translations.literal: Counties (21): Weicheng, Huaili, Hu, Zhouzhi, Tai, Yuyi, Meiyang, Mei, Yong, Qi, Xunyi, Yumi, Chencang, Duyang, Qian, Haozhi,
translations.idiomatic: Counties (21): Weicheng, Huaili, Hu, Zhouzhi, Tai, Yuyi, Meiyang, Mei, Yong, Qi, Xunyi, Yumi, Chencang, Duyang, Qian, Haozhi,

sentences.zh: 虢，安陵，茂陵，平陵，武功，

translations.literal: Guo, Anling, Maoling, Pingling, Wugong,
translations.idiomatic: Additional counties: Guo, Anling, Maoling, Pingling, and Wugong.

sentences.zh: 弘農郡，戶十一萬八千九十一，口四十七萬五千九百五十四。

translations.literal: Hongnong Commandery: households 118,091; population 475,954.
translations.idiomatic: Hongnong Commandery counted 118,091 households and 475,954 people.

sentences.zh: 縣十一：弘農，盧氏，陝，宜陽，黽池，丹水，新安，商，析，陸渾，上雒。

translations.literal: Counties (11): Hongnong, Lushi, Shan, Yiyang, Mianchi, Danshui, Xin'an, Shang, Xi, Luhun, Shangluo.
translations.idiomatic: Counties (11): Hongnong, Lushi, Shan, Yiyang, Mianchi, Danshui, Xin'an, Shang, Xi, Luhun, and Shangluo.

sentences.zh: 河東郡，戶二十三萬六千八百九十六，口九十六萬二千九百一十二。

translations.literal: Hedong Commandery: households 236,896; population 962,912.
translations.idiomatic: Hedong Commandery had 236,896 households and 962,912 people.

sentences.zh: 縣二十四：安邑，大陽，猗氏，解，蒲反，河北，左邑，汾陰，聞喜，濩澤，端氏，臨汾，垣，皮氏，長脩，平陽，襄陵，彘，楊，北屈，蒲子，絳，狐讘，騏。

translations.literal: Counties (24): Anyi, Dayang, Yishi, Xie, Pufan, Hebei, Zuoyi, Fenyin, Wenxi, Huoze, Duanshi, Linfen, Yuan, Pishi, Changxiu, Pingyang, Xiangling, Zhi, Yang, Beiqu, Puzi, Jiang, Husheni, Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Counties (24): Anyi, Dayang, Yishi, Xie, Pufan, Hebei, Zuoyi, Fenyin, Wenxi, Huoze, Duanshi, Linfen, Yuan, Pishi, Changxiu, Pingyang, Xiangling, Zhi, Yang, Beiqu, Puzi, Jiang, Husheni, and Qi.

sentences.zh: 太原郡，戶十六萬九千八百六十三，口六十八萬四百八十八。
